title: Initial implementation and MVP
body:
# Initial implementation and MVP

_Originally titled "Writ substrate & the quest/topic writ type". Renamed 2026-04-15 and re-parented under `w-mo0ffbff` (Improvements to the quest system) — now scoped as the sub-umbrella for "what shipped in v1 and what substrate work remains," complementing the sibling trees for workflow/orientation and vocabulary._

_Imported from `.scratch/conversation-topics-as-writs.md` (2026-04-10)._

## Goal

Establish a durable, queryable substrate for capturing inquiry and session continuity — one that lives in the guild's books rather than in Coco's scratch files, and is visible to autonomous planning agents. Concretely: a writ type (`quest`) that uses the existing writ infrastructure (tree, lifecycle, links, CDC, CLI, UI) without triggering execution, because Spider only dispatches writ types explicitly mapped to a rig template. Unmapped types are inert-by-construction.

## Status

Mostly shipped — v1 landed as the `quest` writ type. Eight sub-questions remain open as child quests. v2 (structured decisions & ratification) deferred. A structural reframe (file-canonical bodies, T1.8) emerged 2026-04-10 and would dissolve several siblings if pursued.

## Next Steps

No active work on the root inquiry. When any child quest progresses, reflect the delta in Context here. The most consequential open thread is now T1.8 (file-canonical quest bodies) — if pursued, it likely closes T1.6, T1.7, and most of T1.3 in a single structural shift, and partially closes T1.4 via T1.9.

## Context

The load-bearing insight is that Spider's dispatch is opt-in per writ type via the rig-template mapping. A writ type with no mapping is inert — it uses the substrate for storage and visibility but nothing tries to execute it. No special-case branches in Clerk or Spider were needed; the mapping table already enforces it. This makes the "parasitic" writ type pattern cheap to repeat for any future state we want queryable without execution.

v1 scope was deliberately narrow: declare the type, establish a body template, update Coco's startup/wrap-up to use it, migrate prior scratch content. Shipped and in active use. Two bonus resolutions fell out of the work: `nsg writ edit` already handles body edits on non-draft writs (so the workflow doesn't need a new Clerk endpoint), and the writ-vocabulary docs were updated to frame writ as a typed substrate rather than as obligation-flavored-only.

A second-order observation surfaced 2026-04-10 after a day of practice: the *single mutable body row* model collapses three structurally different layers (metadata, synthesized narrative, timeline of events) into one row, which is the root cause of the editing friction in T1.6 and the audit-trail loss in T1.7. T1.8 proposes the structural fix; T1.9 captures the timeline-layer question that fix raises.

Eight distinct sub-questions are deferred into child quests rather than forced into v1:

- **Plugin-contributed writ types** (T1.2) — currently on the `guild.json` escape hatch; the `contributes` manifest pattern remains undesigned.
- **Concurrent session writes** (T1.3) — last-write-wins risk on a shared quest body, unmitigated. Likely closed by T1.8.
- **Decisions & ratification** (T1.4) — the v2 sketch. Decisions currently live as prose in quest bodies, unqueryable. Partially closed by T1.9.
- **Naming** (T1.5) — "quest" doesn't sit right; exploring `sketch` / `jotting` / `scribble`.
- **Editing ergonomics** (T1.6) — `nsg writ edit --body "<heredoc>"` is heavier than editing a markdown file. **Superseded by T1.8** (file-canonical dissolves the editing problem entirely).
- **Body edit history** (T1.7) — quest bodies are mutable rows with no audit trail. Likely closed by T1.8 (git-on-file while live, immutable DB snapshot when archived).
- **File-canonical quest bodies** (T1.8) — *new, 2026-04-10.* Restructure storage so the body lives in a file in the guild and the writ row is metadata + cache. Three constraints fixed (file canonical, quests only, files in the guild). Three open design questions: location/partitioning, status-driven file lifecycle, slug/rename handling. Likely closes T1.3, T1.6, T1.7, and partially T1.4.
- **Event-log layer for quest writs** (T1.9) — *new, 2026-04-10.* Where does the timeline of quest events (status changes, child spawns, decisions, edit messages) live — side-table or markdown section? Held until T1.8 advances since the file/DB split changes what the event log is *for*.

## References

- `.claude/skills/quests/SKILL.md` — the shipped workflow definition
- Child quests:
  - T1.2 plugin-contributed types — w-mnsww5kg-6bcbc7db1d4e
  - T1.3 concurrent writes — w-mnswwgah-7dca55bc359e
  - T1.4 decisions & ratification — w-mnswwzdv-88c29d29f84b
  - T1.5 naming — w-mnsx3onr-6e9e21cd3f30
  - T1.6 editing ergonomics — w-mnszjvrr-87e44398b1e9 (superseded by T1.8)
  - T1.7 body edit history — w-mnszkcd5-1f3e2acb4f4a
  - T1.8 file-canonical bodies — w-mnt0jin1-960d83b73712
  - T1.9 event-log layer — w-mnt0i31e-1a1d46cb59a7
- Original design doc: `.scratch/conversation-topics-as-writs.md` (source of import)
- Reference shapes for v2: Astrolabe `Decision` / `DecisionAnalysis` types; Spider `InputRequestDoc` + `patron-input` block
- GSD body-template inspiration: previously cached at `.scratch/gsd-research/` (now superseded)

## Notes

- 2026-04-10: opened, T1 hierarchy imported, v1 already shipped at the time of import.
- 2026-04-10: T1.6 (editing ergonomics) and T1.7 (body edit history) spawned from one-day meta review of the substrate.
- 2026-04-10: T1.8 (file-canonical bodies) and T1.9 (event-log layer) spawned later same session — structural reframe that emerged from the editing-friction diagnosis. T1.8 supersedes the design within T1.6.

items.title: Decisions & ratification (v2 of the writ-substrate design)
Concurrent session writes to the same quest body
